content.problemStatement:
Given an integer **N** representing the length of the array, followed by **N elements** of the array,Find the longest subarray that does not contain zero.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN, denoting the length of the input array.
Second line of input contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output on a single line - the length of longest non-zero subarray.
content.inputFormat: First line contains N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers - the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the length of the longest contiguous subarray that does not contain the value zero.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array contains three non-zero elements each isolated by zeros, so the longest subarray without a zero has length 1.
After the zero at position 3 the subarray [3,4,5] is the longest contiguous segment without a zero, length 3.
All elements are zero, therefore there is no non-zero subarray; the answer is 0.

solution.approach: The key insight is to use a sliding window approach to track the longest subarray without zeros. We maintain two pointers, start and end, representing the current window. When we encounter a zero, we move the start pointer to the next element. We keep track of the maximum length of the subarray without zeros seen so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a few variables to keep track of the window and the maximum length.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int n; scanf("%d",&n); int maxLen=0,cur=0; for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int x; scanf("%d",&x); if(x==0) cur=0; else cur++; if(cur>maxLen) maxLen=cur;} printf("%d\n",maxLen); return 0; }
code.JavaScript: const lines = require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split('\n'); let i=0; const n = +lines[i++]; const arr = lines[i++].trim().split(' ').map(Number); let maxLen = 0, cur = 0; for(let x of arr){ if(x === 0) cur = 0; else cur++; if(cur > maxLen) maxLen = cur; } console.log(String(maxLen));
